response_raw:
1. Rider objective & context
A single off-road motorcycle rider is traversing a network of dirt tracks across an open, hilly landscape. The footage appears to be captured from a drone or elevated static camera position filming the rider moving through the terrain. The timestamp on all frames reads 17:04.

2. Rider
The rider is visible as a small dark figure throughout the early-to-mid frames, wearing dark clothing. Due to the significant viewing distance and image resolution, no specific body position, gear details, or clothing colours beyond dark/black can be confirmed. The rider appears upright on the motorcycle in the frames where both are distinguishable. In later frames the rider becomes progressively smaller and less discernible as the camera pans or the rider moves further away.

3. Terrain & environment
- Open, semi-arid hillside terrain with dry, sandy/dirt soil
- Multiple intersecting dirt tracks are visible, with heavy rutting indicating repeated prior use
- Scattered low scrub vegetation, small bushes, and sparse young pine/conifer trees
- Exposed pale limestone or similar rock outcroppings visible in the foreground of several frames
- Rolling hills with a tree line (predominantly conifers) along the ridgeline in the background
- Sky transitions from partly cloudy with blue sky in earlier frames to heavier, darker overcast cloud cover in later frames; one frame shows a dramatic dark storm cloud over a distant mountain range
- No visible standing water, mud, or snow on the track surface

4. Events
- Frames 1–3: Rider is positioned mid-frame, moving generally downhill/toward the camera along a central dirt track
- Frames 4–5: Rider continues moving, camera appears to pan or the viewing angle shifts, revealing more of the track network to the right; rider moves toward the left side of frame
- Frames 6–9: Rider has moved further left and is now smaller in frame, continuing along a narrower section of track on the left hillside; camera continues to pan
- Frame 10 (white circles visible): Two circular UI elements appear on screen, suggesting the video player scrubber or touch interaction; rider is still faintly visible upper-left
- Frames 11–12: Rider is a very small dark speck on the left hillside track; the broader track network dominates the frame
- Frame 13: Camera view cuts to a wide panoramic shot of a distant mountain range under heavy dark storm clouds; rider not visible
- Frame 14: Camera angle has shifted significantly to a close-up of a sandy slope with green scrubland; rider not visible
- Frame 15: Camera shows a different hillside section with lighter, greener grass and a dirt track curving through; rider not visible
- Frame 16 (final): Wide establishing shot of the full track network from a new angle, showing the complete hillside layout; rider not visible; two circular UI scrubber elements visible

5. Outcome
The rider remains upright and on the motorcycle throughout all frames in which they are visible. No crash, fall, or stoppage is observable. The rider progressively moves further from the camera until they are no longer distinguishable. The clip ends with wide terrain shots in which the rider is absent from view.

6. Unclear or not visible
- Rider's helmet, gear colour/brand, and specific body positioning are not discernible due to viewing distance and image resolution
- Motorcycle make, model, or colour cannot be confirmed
- Rider speed cannot be determined from still frames
- Whether the rider is alone or part of a group is not confirmed; no other riders are clearly visible
- The purpose or nature of the session (recreational, competitive, practice) is not visible
- The white circular elements appearing in several frames appear to be video player UI elements (scrubber/touch targets); their exact function is not confirmed
- The camera source (drone, fixed elevated camera, or handheld at distance) is not visible
- What happens to the rider after they pass out of frame is not visible
